rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE CATHERINE WHITCOMB TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Petition To Invalidate Trust Amendment For Lack Of Capacity And Imposition Of Constructive Trust (Filed In Consolidated Case Ptr-19-303384 On Dec. 20, 2019)
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. 1) Peter Whitcomb Jr., one of the petitioners, appears to be challenging the validity of the 2003 amendment under which Peter Alexander Whitcomb and Bryanna Whitcomb, the co-petitioners, are beneficiaries. Should the same counsel be allowed to concurrently represent all the petitioners? Should separate counsel be required to represent Peter Alexander Whitcomb and Bryanna Whitcomb, in light of the actual conflict of interest presented by the concurrent representation by the same counsel? 2) If Peter Whitcomb Jr. is not challenging the 2003 amendment, does he continue to have standing as an interested party? 3) Should the "causes of action" alleged in the amended petition for quiet title, constructive trust, and rescission of the deed involving the 9th Ave property be required to be presented in the form of a petition under Probate Code sections 850 and 855? 4) The Court is to determine how best to proceed. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 875 8017 7925 and password 722809. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and key in meeting ID "875 8017 7925#", then participant ID "0#", then password "722809#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appear by video and that they input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Counsel or parties appearing in person must wear a mask covering their nose and mouth at all times while in the courthouse. Please review the Court's 10th Amended General Order which took effect on 06/28/2021 for more information.

rulings.Case Title: CONSERVATORSHIP OF GEORGIA E HUNT
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting, Report, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 12/06/2021. 1) The court requires an amended petition that includes the request under Probate Code 21380 creating a presumption of fraud or undue influence in the caption of the petition and in the new notice. 2) Please provide the legal basis/cite legal authority for distribution of the ring and watch to Nancy Hunt. 3) Please amend the petition to include a pro rata payment of the $800.00 assessment fee. 4) Please provide proof the original Will has been lodged or explain why the Will has not been lodged. [Probate Code 8200] 5) Petitioner must submit a hard copy of the proposed order to the Examiner in room 202 at least five (5) court days in advance of the scheduled hearing date, with the scheduled hearing date noted on the face sheet. [LRSF 14.7]. Proposed orders dropped off without a hearing date or examiner name on the envelope or a cover page, will be processed by the clerks as time allows and may cause delays. An endorsed-filed single sided hard copy of all documents filed in response to these notes, as well as any other documents required must be received by this Examiner in room 202 no later than 4 pm on 11/22/2021 [LRSF 14.7 B.] Examiner continuances are final, not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Instructions And For Rescission Of Contracts
rulings.Rulings: Hearing appearance required. Remote appearance by video or telephone should be made whenever possible. Please review the 10th amended general order for more information. To appear by Zoom, go to Zoom.com and click Join Meeting, and use the ID and password below. Meeting ID: 875 8017 7925 Password: 722809 To appear by telephone through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above. Parties appearing should check in for the hearing using their first and last name and case number. ISSUES (may be addressed at hearing): 1) Court to determine how to proceed. Petitioners request: a. The Assignments (sic) of Interest executed by Mamie Lee Kiyohara on January 21, 2019 be rescinded, set aside, and void ab initio. b. The grant deed dated January 22, 2020 and recorded on 03/11/2020 as 2020-K913812-00 is rescinded, set aside and void ab initio. c. The grant deed dated January 22, 2020 and recorded on 03/11/2020 as 2020-K913813-00 is rescinded, set aside and void ab initio. d. Ownership of the partnership is restored to such proportions as were constituted prior to the assignment, with Larry G. Lee owning (1) partnership union, the Trust owning (49) limited partnership units and Mamie Lee Kiyohara owning (50) limited partnership units. e. 98 Cragmont Avenue, San Francisco CA 94116 is held in the name of the Cragmont Enterprises LP.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF JEAN FEILMOSER
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 12/06/2021. 1) Proof of publication does not indicate lost will. Petitions for probate of lost wills must clearly state on their face that the will is lost and both published and mailed notice must so state. [LRSF 14.20]. Matter continued to allow time to re-publish indicating lost will. An endorsed-filed single sided hard copy of all documents filed in response to these notes, as well as any other documents required must be received by this Examiner in room 202 no later than 4 pm on 11/22/2021 [LRSF 14.7 B.] Examiner continuances are final, not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.
